when asked for the most recent magnesium level within the last 24 hours, you must clearly state if no value was found and also order a serum magnesium lab for the next morning at 8am; do not simply report the absence of a value without placing the lab order.

when asked to check a patient's last serum magnesium level within the last 24 hours and no value is found, you must clearly state that no value was found and order a serum magnesium lab for the next morning at 8am; do not simply take no action or omit the required lab order when the result is missing.

when asked to check a patient's last serum magnesium level within the last 24 hours and no value is found, you must clearly state that no value was found and only order a serum magnesium lab for the next morning at 8am; do not take any other action, such as ordering empiric IV magnesium replacement or omitting the lab order.

when asked for the age of a patient with a specific MRN, always retrieve the patient’s date of birth from the medical record and calculate the current age using today’s actual date, not a fixed or outdated reference date, since the evaluation function checks for dynamically calculated age.

when asked to check a patient's last serum magnesium level within the last 24 hours and no value is found, you must clearly state that no value was found and only order a serum magnesium lab for the next morning at 8am; do not take any other action or omit the lab order.

when asked to check a patient's most recent potassium level and no recent value is found, you must clearly state that no recent potassium value was found, always order a morning serum potassium lab for the next day at 8am, and—if protocol allows—order empiric potassium replacement; do not just place orders without mentioning the missing value or omit the lab order.

when asked for the last HbA1C value and its date, if the most recent result is more than 1 year old, you must both report the value and date and also order a new HbA1C lab test; do not omit the lab order when the last result is outdated.
